definition: A waste product from muscle metabolism filtered by the kidneys. Blood creatinine level reflects kidney function.
low_interpretation: Low creatinine usually indicates lower muscle mass rather than a kidney problem. Can occur with aging, low protein intake, or liver disease. Generally less concerning than high creatinine.
high_interpretation: Elevated creatinine may indicate kidney disease, dehydration, or high protein intake. Warrants follow-up with your healthcare provider.
recommendations_low: Eat adequate protein (lean meats, fish, legumes); engage in moderate strength training; stay hydrated. Discuss with your provider.
recommendations_high: Stay hydrated; avoid excessive protein; follow your provider's advice; monitor blood pressure and blood sugar.

definition: A protein in red blood cells that carries oxygen throughout the body. Essential for energy and vitality.
low_interpretation: Low hemoglobin suggests anemia—reduced oxygen-carrying capacity. Can cause fatigue, weakness, shortness of breath, dizziness.
high_interpretation: High hemoglobin can occur with dehydration, lung disease, or living at high altitude.
recommendations_low: Increase iron-rich foods (leafy greens, lean red meat, beans, lentils); ensure adequate vitamin B12 and folate; discuss supplements with your provider.
recommendations_high: Stay hydrated; avoid smoking; follow your provider's guidance.

definition: A simple sugar that is the body's main energy source. Fasting blood glucose is used to screen for diabetes.
low_interpretation: Low blood sugar (hypoglycemia) can cause shakiness, sweating, confusion, weakness. May occur with certain medications or irregular eating.
high_interpretation: High fasting glucose may suggest prediabetes or diabetes. Requires follow-up testing and lifestyle changes.
recommendations_low: Eat regular meals; include complex carbs and protein; avoid skipping meals; discuss medication adjustments with your provider.
recommendations_high: Reduce refined sugars and simple carbs; increase fiber; exercise regularly (150 min/week); lose weight if advised; take prescribed medications; monitor blood sugar.

definition: Low-density lipoprotein, often called 'bad' cholesterol. Carries cholesterol to arteries; high levels increase cardiovascular disease risk.
low_interpretation: Low LDL is generally desirable and associated with lower cardiovascular risk.
high_interpretation: High LDL significantly increases risk of heart attack and stroke. Often managed with diet, exercise, and sometimes statin medication.
recommendations_low: Maintain heart-healthy habits: balanced diet, regular exercise, avoid smoking.
recommendations_high: Reduce saturated and trans fats (red meat, butter, fried foods); increase soluble fiber (oats, beans, fruits); eat omega-3-rich fish; exercise regularly; lose weight if overweight; discuss statins with your provider if recommended.

definition: High-density lipoprotein, often called 'good' cholesterol. Helps remove LDL from arteries; higher levels are protective.
low_interpretation: Low HDL is a risk factor for heart disease.
high_interpretation: High HDL is generally protective for heart health.
recommendations_low: Exercise regularly (especially aerobic); quit smoking; include healthy fats (olive oil, avocados, nuts); moderate alcohol may help some (ask your provider).
recommendations_high: Maintain your healthy habits.

definition: The sum of LDL, HDL, and other lipoproteins. Used with LDL/HDL to assess cardiovascular risk.
low_interpretation: Low total cholesterol is uncommon; usually not concerning.
high_interpretation: High total cholesterol (200+) increases cardiovascular risk. Focus on LDL and HDL for actionable guidance.
recommendations_low: Maintain balanced diet and exercise.
recommendations_high: Same as LDL: reduce saturated/trans fats; increase fiber; exercise; lose weight; discuss statins with your provider.

definition: A type of fat in the blood. High levels increase heart disease risk, especially with low HDL.
low_interpretation: Low triglycerides are generally not concerning.
high_interpretation: High triglycerides increase cardiovascular risk and may indicate metabolic syndrome.
recommendations_low: Maintain healthy habits.
recommendations_high: Reduce refined carbs and sugar; limit alcohol; exercise regularly; lose weight; increase omega-3s (fatty fish); discuss medication with your provider if needed.

definition: Estimated glomerular filtration rate. Reflects how well your kidneys filter waste.
low_interpretation: Below 60 may indicate chronic kidney disease. 60–89 may be early or age-related.
high_interpretation: High eGFR is generally not concerning.
recommendations_low: Control blood pressure and blood sugar; avoid NSAIDs when possible; stay hydrated; follow a kidney-friendly diet if advised; see a nephrologist if recommended.
recommendations_high: Continue healthy habits.

definition: Thyroid-stimulating hormone. Regulates thyroid function; high TSH suggests underactive thyroid, low suggests overactive.
low_interpretation: Low TSH may indicate hyperthyroidism (overactive thyroid)—fatigue, weight loss, racing heart.
high_interpretation: High TSH may indicate hypothyroidism (underactive thyroid)—fatigue, weight gain, cold sensitivity.
recommendations_low: Follow your provider's treatment plan; may need medication or further testing.
recommendations_high: Thyroid medication (e.g., levothyroxine) and follow-up; ensure adequate iodine if deficient; avoid excess soy if on medication.

definition: Blood urea nitrogen. Waste product from protein metabolism; filtered by kidneys.
low_interpretation: Low BUN can occur with low protein intake, liver disease, or overhydration.
high_interpretation: Elevated BUN can suggest kidney dysfunction or dehydration.
recommendations_low: Ensure adequate protein in diet; discuss with provider if persistent.
recommendations_high: Stay hydrated; avoid excessive protein; follow provider's advice.

definition: Hemoglobin A1c. Reflects average blood glucose over 2–3 months. Key test for diabetes diagnosis and management.
low_interpretation: Low HbA1c is uncommon; may occur with anemia or recent blood loss.
high_interpretation: Elevated HbA1c suggests prediabetes (5.7–6.4%) or diabetes (6.5%+). Important to manage.
recommendations_low: Discuss with your provider.
recommendations_high: Reduce refined carbs; exercise 150+ min/week; lose weight if advised; take prescribed medications (metformin, etc.); monitor blood sugar; follow a diabetes care plan.

definition: Essential for bone health, immune function, and mood. Many people are deficient.
low_interpretation: Low Vitamin D can affect bone health, immunity, and mood. Common in northern climates and indoor lifestyles.
high_interpretation: Very high Vitamin D from excess supplementation can be harmful.
recommendations_low: Get safe sun exposure (15–20 min, 2–3x/week); eat fatty fish, fortified milk; consider Vitamin D supplement (discuss dose with your provider).
recommendations_high: Reduce supplementation; follow your provider's guidance.

definition: Essential for nerve function and red blood cell formation. Deficiency can cause anemia and nerve issues.
low_interpretation: Low B12 can cause fatigue, weakness, numbness, memory problems. Common in vegetarians and older adults.
high_interpretation: High B12 is uncommon; often from supplements.
recommendations_low: Eat B12-rich foods (meat, fish, eggs, dairy); consider B12 supplement or injections; discuss with your provider.
recommendations_high: Usually no action needed; discuss with provider if concerned.

definition: White blood cell count. Measures immune cells that fight infection.
low_interpretation: Low WBC may suggest bone marrow or immune issues; increases infection risk.
high_interpretation: Elevated WBC may suggest infection or inflammation.
recommendations_low: Follow up with your provider; avoid unnecessary infections; good hygiene.
recommendations_high: Treat underlying cause; follow provider's advice.

definition: Red blood cell count. Carries oxygen; low levels indicate anemia.
low_interpretation: Low RBC indicates anemia—fatigue, weakness, pale skin.
high_interpretation: High RBC can occur with dehydration or lung/heart conditions.
recommendations_low: Increase iron, B12, folate; treat underlying cause; discuss with provider.
recommendations_high: Stay hydrated; follow provider's guidance.

definition: Cell fragments that help blood clot. Essential for wound healing.
low_interpretation: Low platelets increase bleeding risk.
high_interpretation: High platelets may increase clotting risk.
recommendations_low: Avoid activities that cause injury; follow provider's plan.
recommendations_high: Follow provider's advice; may need further testing.

definition: Alanine aminotransferase, a liver enzyme. Elevated levels suggest liver injury.
low_interpretation: Low ALT is typically not concerning.
high_interpretation: Elevated ALT often indicates liver injury, fatty liver, or hepatitis.
recommendations_low: No action typically needed.
recommendations_high: Limit or avoid alcohol; avoid acetaminophen excess; maintain healthy weight; follow provider's plan; consider hepatitis screening.

definition: Aspartate aminotransferase, an enzyme in liver, heart, and muscle.
low_interpretation: Low AST is typically not concerning.
high_interpretation: Elevated AST can indicate liver or muscle damage. Often interpreted with ALT.
recommendations_low: No action typically needed.
recommendations_high: Same as ALT: limit alcohol; healthy weight; follow provider's plan.

definition: An electrolyte essential for heart rhythm and muscle function.
low_interpretation: Low potassium can cause weakness, cramps, irregular heartbeat.
high_interpretation: High potassium can affect heart rhythm; requires prompt attention.
recommendations_low: Eat potassium-rich foods (bananas, potatoes, spinach); discuss supplements with provider.
recommendations_high: Limit high-potassium foods; avoid certain supplements; follow provider's plan.

definition: An electrolyte that helps regulate fluid balance and blood pressure.
low_interpretation: Low sodium can cause confusion, weakness, seizures.
high_interpretation: High sodium often from dehydration; can affect blood pressure.
recommendations_low: Follow provider's guidance; may need fluid restriction or treatment.
recommendations_high: Stay hydrated; reduce salt intake; follow provider's advice.

definition: Essential for bones, teeth, and nerve function.
low_interpretation: Low calcium can affect bones and nerves.
high_interpretation: High calcium may indicate parathyroid or other issues.
recommendations_low: Eat calcium-rich foods (dairy, leafy greens); discuss supplements with provider.
recommendations_high: Follow provider's guidance; limit calcium supplements if advised.

definition: Stores iron in the body. Low levels indicate iron deficiency.
low_interpretation: Low ferritin indicates iron deficiency—fatigue, weakness, hair loss.
high_interpretation: High ferritin can indicate inflammation or iron overload.
recommendations_low: Eat iron-rich foods; take iron supplement if advised; pair with vitamin C for absorption.
recommendations_high: Follow provider's plan; may need to limit iron intake.

definition: C-reactive protein. Marker of inflammation; high-sensitivity CRP used for cardiovascular risk.
low_interpretation: Low CRP suggests low inflammation.
high_interpretation: High CRP suggests inflammation from infection, arthritis, or cardiovascular risk.
recommendations_low: Maintain healthy lifestyle.
recommendations_high: Address underlying cause; improve diet and exercise; quit smoking; follow provider's plan.
